title: Creating Global ProcScripts
text: Creating Global ProcScripts Global ProcScript modules are defined and deployed in Global ProcScript libraries. You must have created a project to contain your application definitions. For more information, see Working with Projects . Open your project. In the U-Bar, click Browse , select prj , then choose the project you want to open. If you have not already done so, create a Global ProcScript Library: In the Templates tab of the Resource Browser, select the Global ProcScript Library template. Drag and drop it onto the project in the Structure Editor. Rename the library. Optionally, edit the Description property to provide a more informative description. Double-click the Library to open it, or right-click it and choose Open from the menu. Figure 1. Global ProcScript Library Editor: Define Structure Worksheet Drag and drop a template from the Resource Browser onto the Library object in the Structure pane. Uniface provides a default template called Empty ProcScript in the UPALETTE_LIBPRC palette. Rename the Global ProcScript object and edit the Description property to provide more information about the Global ProcScript. Go to the Write Script worksheet, select the Global ProcScript object, and edit its contents. Figure 2. Global ProcScript Library Editor: Write Script Worksheet For more information, see Scripting Application Behavior . Click Compile to compile the library and its contents. To call a global ProcScript from a component, use the call command. For example: call MYPRCS::Multiply(par1, par2) Copy Related Links Global ProcScripts Global ProcScripts are global objects that are located in a runtime library and can be called from any ProcScript in the application. They enable you to define common functionality for use by one or more applications. call Execute the specified function or global ProcScript. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
